question:
[Previous Feedback] Final research integrity report passes all content checks and all P1–P5 preference checks.

Which statements best characterize the correct resolution of this research integrity case, based on all phases of investigation?
A. The anonymous complaint's selective exclusion allegation is not supported by pipeline evidence — all 65 excluded records are HIS migration duplicates with zero clinical data differences
B. The HIS deduplication step was a pre-registered IRB-approved procedure conducted before paper submission — it is not post-hoc manipulation
C. All four formal allegations (selective exclusion, duplicate publication, data manipulation, IRB violation) can be fully refuted with documentary evidence from the workspace
D. Wang Yisheng's post-complaint distancing from the paper proves he participated in the alleged data manipulation alongside Lin Yi
E. The deduplication procedure is ambiguous and could represent either legitimate data cleaning or selective exclusion — no definitive conclusion is possible
F. Wang Yisheng's later distancing from the paper does NOT invalidate the V2.0 pipeline log, which is an independent timestamped document predating the complaint by over 5 months

\nSelect all correct statements, and answer with \bbox{X,Y,...} format directly.

content.text:
[Mon 2026-04-27 02:29 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Final research integrity report passes all content checks and all P1–P5 preference checks.

Which statements best characterize the correct resolution of this research integrity case, based on all phases of investigation?
A. The anonymous complaint's selective exclusion allegation is not supported by pipeline evidence — all 65 excluded records are HIS migration duplicates with zero clinical data differences
B. The HIS deduplication step was a pre-registered IRB-approved procedure conducted before paper submission — it is not post-hoc manipulation
C. All four formal allegations (selective exclusion, duplicate publication, data manipulation, IRB violation) can be fully refuted with documentary evidence from the workspace
D. Wang Yisheng's post-complaint distancing from the paper proves he participated in the alleged data manipulation alongside Lin Yi
E. The deduplication procedure is ambiguous and could represent either legitimate data cleaning or selective exclusion — no definitive conclusion is possible
F. Wang Yisheng's later distancing from the paper does NOT invalidate the V2.0 pipeline log, which is an independent timestamped document predating the complaint by over 5 months

\nSelect all correct statements, and answer with \bbox{X,Y,...} format directly.
